project.name: iFlyFlat Clone — The Flights Club
project.description: A premium clone and improvement of iflyflat.com.au targeting Australian business owners. Design elevated with Flexfume-inspired animations. Goal is higher conversion, premium feel, and stronger mobile experience.

fonts.display: Canela or Playfair Display — for large premium headlines
fonts.body: Inter or Outfit — clean, readable, modern
fonts.accent: DM Mono — for stats, numbers, labels

stack.optional_cms: Sanity v3 — for case studies and flight examples (add in Phase 3)

animation_system.philosophy: Animate to add weight and drama at key moments only. Not everything moves. Motion should feel earned.

stagger_children.usage: Feature grids, case study cards, stat items

scroll_pinned_text.usage: Large statement text section — each line pins and reveals as user scrolls

app.layout.tsx: Root layout — fonts, metadata, global providers
app.globals.css: Tailwind base, custom scrollbar, root CSS variables

layout.Navbar.tsx: Sticky navigation with transparent-to-solid scroll behavior
layout.Footer.tsx: Full premium footer with columns, legal, social links

sections.HeroSection.tsx: Main hero with parallax background and animated headline
sections.TwoPathsSection.tsx: Concierge vs Membership split card section
sections.WhyDifferentSection.tsx: Six feature blocks with staggered animation
sections.FinalCTASection.tsx: Dark closing CTA with urgency line

ui.CTAButton.tsx: Primary and secondary button variants with hover states
ui.SectionLabel.tsx: Small uppercase label above section titles

lib.animations.ts: Shared Framer Motion variants and GSAP helper functions
lib.constants.ts: All site copy, data arrays, config values
lib.types.ts: TypeScript interfaces for all data shapes

public.images: All static images — hero, flights, member photos
public.fonts: Self-hosted font files if not using Google Fonts

sections.name: Loading Intro
sections.description: Full-screen dark overlay with logo animating in, tagline fading up, then fades out to reveal the page. Runs once per session.

content.logo: The Flights Club by iFLYflat SVG

sections.notes: Use sessionStorage to only show once per browser session. Skip button optional.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.description: Sticky navbar. Starts transparent over hero. Becomes solid dark background on scroll past 80px. Mobile hamburger menu with slide-in panel.
sections.animation: Framer Motion — navbar background opacity transitions on scroll using useScroll hook

sections.name: Hero Section
sections.description: Full viewport height. Dark background with slow parallax image of Business Class cabin. Headline animates in word by word. Two CTAs below. Floating trust badge.

content.subheadline: Turn every dollar you spend into Business Class freedom, exclusive experiences, and a private community of leaders who never fly economy again.

animation.background: GSAP parallax — background image moves at 0.4x scroll speed
animation.headline: Framer Motion — each word fades up with 0.05s stagger delay
animation.subheadline: Framer Motion — fade in after headline completes, delay 0.8s
animation.ctas: Framer Motion — fade in from below, delay 1.2s
animation.trust_badge: Framer Motion — fade in last, delay 1.5s

sections.name: Media Social Proof Strip
sections.description: Thin horizontal strip. Label: TRUSTED AND FEATURED IN. Logos scroll infinitely left in a seamless loop. No duplicate images — use SVG or text-based logos.

animation.library: CSS animation — translate keyframes, duplicated list for seamless loop

sections.name: Scroll-Pinned Statement Text
sections.description: Flexfume-style section. Three massive lines of text. Section pins to the screen as user scrolls. Each line reveals sequentially triggered by scroll progress. Dark background with subtle grain texture.

animation.each_line: opacity 0.15 to 1 at different scroll progress points (0-33%, 33-66%, 66-100%)

sections.name: Two Ways to Fly Smarter
sections.description: Side by side cards. Left: Concierge (one-time). Right: Membership (ongoing). Each card has an image, price/detail, and CTA. Clear visual language for the difference.

cards.label: AD-HOC
cards.title: Points Flight Concierge
cards.description: You already have points. We find and book the Business Class seat for you. One trip, handled completely.

cards.label: ONGOING
cards.title: Flights Club Membership
cards.description: For business owners who want this every year. Ongoing strategy, bookings, and guaranteed Business Class returns.

animation.entrance: Both cards slide in from opposite sides — left card from left, right card from right
animation.hover: Card lifts 4px, border glow with card's accent color

sections.name: Flight Savings Comparison
sections.description: Shows what Business Class seats cost in points vs. retail cash. Three routes displayed as cards. Highlight the gap visually. Add 'what that saving gets you' line.

routes.saving_equivalent: A business laptop, two months of software, or a family weekend away
Two months of office rent or a team offsite trip
A full marketing campaign or new hire onboarding budget

animation.cards: Framer Motion stagger entrance — each card fades up 0.15s after the previous
animation.savings_bar: GSAP — animated bar showing the ratio of points value vs retail cost

sections.name: Animated Stats Bar
sections.description: Full-width dark section. Three stats with labels. Numbers count up from 0 when section enters the viewport. Pure authority signal.

sections.name: Member Case Studies
sections.description: Real member stories with spending level, savings, and business type. Scrollable cards on mobile. Each card shows a short member quote.

members.name: Clara
members.quote: I had no idea my supplier payments were worth this much.

members.name: Sarah & Mitch
members.quote: We fly as a couple now. Business Class, every time.

members.name: Antony
members.quote: My accountant couldn't believe how much we were leaving on the table.

animation.entrance: Framer Motion stagger — cards animate in left to right with 0.15s delay each
animation.hover: Card tilts 1-2deg on hover (Framer Motion rotateY), slight lift and shadow

sections.name: Why The Flights Club is Different
sections.description: Six feature blocks arranged in a 3x2 grid. Each has an icon, title, and 1-2 sentence description. They animate in with stagger. Icons are better quality than original.

features.title: Proven Strategy
features.description: 12+ years of expertise. Billions of points redeemed. A system that delivers results, not promises.

features.title: Concierge Booking
features.description: Our team handles everything — from complex redemptions to securing flatbed seats.

features.title: Exclusive Access
features.description: Closed-group deals, private events, and VIP privileges that aren't available to the public.

features.title: Guaranteed Value
features.description: Members save $5K–$10K per seat every trip, or we keep working until you do.

features.title: Curated Experiences
features.description: Retreats, events, and journeys across seven continents that go well beyond just getting a flight.

features.title: Premium Community
features.description: A curated circle of ambitious business owners who treat travel as a competitive advantage.

animation.icon_animation: Each icon does a subtle scale pulse on section entrance

sections.name: Membership Tiers Preview
sections.description: Three tier cards on the homepage. Enough detail to differentiate them clearly. Each has a 'Learn More' CTA to the full membership page. Black tier gets premium visual treatment.

tiers.name: Explore
tiers.key_benefit: Start turning everyday expenses into Business Class seats
tiers.visual_style: Standard dark card with blue accent border

tiers.name: Platinum
tiers.key_benefit: Guaranteed Business Class flights every year
tiers.visual_style: Slightly elevated card, orange accent border, badge: MOST POPULAR

tiers.name: Black
tiers.target: High-spenders wanting $100K+ in premium travel value
tiers.key_benefit: VIP experiences, private events, and elite-level access
tiers.visual_style: Dark charcoal card with gold border, shimmer animation, badge: ELITE

animation.entrance: Framer Motion — three cards stagger in from bottom
animation.black_card_shimmer: CSS animation — diagonal gold shimmer sweep on hover

sections.name: Podcast Section
sections.description: Showcase the Always Business Class podcast. Three most recent episodes. Drives authority and gives search engines real content.

animation.hover: Card lifts slightly, play button scales up

sections.name: FAQ Section
sections.description: Accordion FAQ. Reduces sales friction by answering objections before they stop a conversion. Structured data markup added for Google FAQ rich results.

questions.q: What points programs do you work with?
questions.a: We work with Qantas Frequent Flyer, Velocity, American Express Membership Rewards, and several bank transfer partners. Our team finds the best program for your situation.

questions.q: Do I need a lot of existing points to get started?
questions.a: Not necessarily. The Concierge service requires you already have points. The Flights Club Membership is about building and strategically using your points over time — we help you accumulate them through your existing business spend.
Any Australian business spending $300K+ annually on cards or eligible expenses. Sole traders, companies, and trusts all qualify.

questions.q: What is the difference between Concierge and Membership?
questions.a: Concierge is a one-off service — you have points, we book your seat. Membership is an ongoing relationship where we build your strategy, manage your points, and guarantee Business Class seats every year.

questions.q: How does the guaranteed value promise work?
questions.a: If your membership doesn't deliver at least $5,000–$10,000 in value per Business Class seat, we keep working until it does. We stand behind the numbers.

questions.q: Can my partner or family use the membership?
questions.a: Yes. Depending on your tier, membership benefits can extend to travel companions. Our team will walk you through what applies to your situation.

animation.accordion_open: Framer Motion height animation — smooth expand/collapse
animation.entrance: Framer Motion — questions fade up on scroll

sections.name: Final CTA Section
sections.description: Clean, dark closing section. One headline, one CTA button, one urgency line. No clutter. Makes the last scroll stop count.

content.headline: Stop Leaving Business Class on the Table
content.subheadline: Your business is already spending the money. The only question is whether you're getting anything back from it.
content.urgency_line: Membership applications are reviewed monthly. Spots are limited per intake.

animation.headline: Framer Motion — word by word fade up
animation.cta_button: Pulse glow animation on button after 3 seconds on screen

sections.name: Footer
sections.description: Premium footer with logo, three link columns, social links, media strip, and legal. Replaces the current minimal footer.

legal.copyright: © 2025 The Flights Club by iFLYflat. All rights reserved.

sections.as_seen_in_strip: Thin text row: AS SEEN IN: 9 News · The Australian · Sky News · SBS · 7 News · 10 News

pages.title: The Flights Club by iFLYflat — Never Fly Economy Again
pages.description: Australia's premier Business Class travel membership for business owners. Turn everyday expenses into guaranteed flat-bed flights and VIP experiences.

pages.title: Membership Tiers — Explore, Platinum, Black | The Flights Club
pages.description: Choose the membership that fits your business spend. From $300K to $5M+, we have a tier that turns your expenses into Business Class freedom.

pages.title: Points Flight Concierge — Book Business Class With Your Points
pages.description: Already have Qantas or Velocity points? Our expert team finds and books your Business Class seat. One trip, handled completely. $1,900 per return seat.

phases.name: Clone and Fix
phases.goal: Get all 14 homepage sections built with static content and basic Framer Motion entrance animations
tasks: Initialise Next.js 14 project with TypeScript and Tailwind
Install Framer Motion, GSAP, GSAP ScrollTrigger, Lucide React
Set up color system and font variables in globals.css and tailwind.config
Build Hero section with word-by-word animation
Build Media logos infinite scroll strip
phases.deliverable: Fully structured homepage — all sections present, entrance animations working

phases.name: Add the Power
phases.goal: Add the premium animation layer that makes this feel like a high-end product
tasks: Build LoadingIntro component with Framer Motion sequence
Build GSAP scroll-pinned Statement Text section
Implement GSAP counter animations on Stats section
Add hover states to all cards (lift, glow, tilt)
Implement Black tier shimmer animation on Membership Tiers
Add parallax effect to Hero background
Build Podcast section with episode cards
Add urgency pulse animation to Final CTA button
phases.deliverable: Full premium animation experience — Flexfume-level visual quality

phases.name: Polish and Launch
phases.goal: Production-ready — performance, SEO, mobile, and full page set
tasks: Full mobile responsiveness audit and fixes
Performance pass — WebP images, lazy loading, font optimisation
Optional: Integrate Sanity CMS for case studies and podcast episodes

install_commands.dependencies: npm install framer-motion gsap @gsap/react lucide-react clsx tailwind-merge

notes_for_copilot: Always use 'use client' directive on components that use Framer Motion or GSAP hooks
Wrap GSAP ScrollTrigger setup inside useLayoutEffect with a gsap.context() for proper cleanup
Use Framer Motion's whileInView with once: true for all section entrance animations — don't replay on scroll back
All images should use Next.js <Image> component with WebP format and explicit width/height
Tailwind classes only — no inline style objects unless absolutely necessary for GSAP targets
Keep all copy and data in /lib/constants.ts — never hardcode content inside components
All components should be typed with TypeScript interfaces from /lib/types.ts
The Loading Intro should check sessionStorage before rendering — skip on repeat visits
The Navbar background transition uses Framer Motion useScroll + useTransform for smooth opacity
Mobile breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px) — always design mobile first
